summary: Iteration 21: deviceId Race Condition Fix - All 8 backend API tests passed (100%). All 6 critical frontend code fixes verified. The root cause (scripts created with user_id='default' when initializeUser() hasn't completed) has been properly addressed.
test_credentials: TEST_device_*, TEST_upload_*, TEST_real_* prefixed IDs for isolation
seed_data_creation: Test scripts created and cleaned up automatically
context_for_next_testing_agent: deviceId race condition fix verified. Expo React Native app (not browser-testable). Backend APIs functional at https://script-recovery-1.preview.emergentagent.com. All script CRUD operations now properly use getDeviceId() directly from AsyncStorage.

CRITICAL_FIX_1_createScript.note: createScript now calls getDeviceId() directly to read from AsyncStorage

CRITICAL_FIX_2_fetchScripts.before: const { deviceId } = get() with fallback to 'default'
CRITICAL_FIX_2_fetchScripts.note: fetchScripts now calls getDeviceId() directly to read from AsyncStorage

CRITICAL_FIX_3_upload_imports.code:
import AsyncStorage from '@react-native-async-storage/async-storage';
import * as Device from 'expo-device';

CRITICAL_FIX_4_upload_getDeviceId.code: const getDeviceId = async () => AsyncStorage.getItem('device_id') || generate new
CRITICAL_FIX_4_upload_getDeviceId.note: Same logic as scriptStore.ts getDeviceId for consistency

CRITICAL_FIX_5_FormData_user_id.code: const userId = await getDeviceId(); formData.append('user_id', userId);

CRITICAL_FIX_6_base64_user_id.code: user_id: userId in base64 fallback JSON payload

backend_flow_tests.description: POST /api/scripts with user_id returns script with matching user_id
GET /api/scripts?user_id=X returns only scripts for that user_id
Create script → script appears in library with same deviceId
POST /api/scripts/upload with user_id in FormData saves correctly
Scripts NOT created with user_id='default' when proper ID provided

root_cause_analysis.problem: Scripts created with user_id='default' but library queries with real deviceId, finding nothing
root_cause_analysis.cause: deviceId race condition - initializeUser() is async, so when user navigates to upload before it completes, store.deviceId is null
root_cause_analysis.solution: All script operations (createScript, fetchScripts, upload) now call getDeviceId() directly from AsyncStorage instead of relying on store state
root_cause_analysis.verification: Backend tests confirm scripts created with specific user_id appear in library fetch with same user_id
